prediction.summary: Multiple failed login attempts were observed across SSH and web services for the 'user@corp' account from various source IPs. This activity culminated in a successful login via both web and SSH from IP 198.51.100.123. An audit system subsequently detected distributed credential reuse across 8 distinct source IPs for the compromised account, strongly indicating a credential stuffing attack.

timeline.event: Failed SSH login for user@corp from 198.51.100.12

Failed SSH login for user@corp from 198.51.100.44
Failed SSH login for user@corp from 198.51.100.88
Failed SSH login for user@corp from 198.51.100.101
Successful SSH login for user@corp from 198.51.100.123
Distributed credential reuse detected across 8 source IPs for account user@corp

recommendations: Immediately disable or reset the password for the 'user@corp' account and force a password change for the user.
Investigate all activities performed by 'user@corp' after the successful logins at 18:22:11 (web) and 18:22:12 (SSH) from 198.51.100.123 to identify potential data exfiltration or further compromise.
Block all identified malicious source IPs (198.51.100.12, .33, .44, .55, .66, .77, .88, .99, .101, .112, .123) at the perimeter firewall.
Implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all user accounts, especially for critical services like SSH and web applications.
Review and strengthen password policies, considering the use of a password manager and regular password rotation for users.
Scan for 'user@corp' credentials in public data breaches to understand the source of the compromised credentials.
